reasoning: As of February 28, 2026, the Oklahoma City Thunder lead the Northwest Division with a 46-15 record, while the Denver Nuggets have fallen to 37-23. This puts the Nuggets 8.5 games behind the Thunder, with only 21-22 games remaining in the regular season. Making up an 8.5-game deficit over ~22 games is practically impossible, as it would require an almost perfect run by the Nuggets and a massive collapse by the Thunder. Furthermore, the betting market priced the Nuggets' chances at just 0.02 on February 19, when the standings gap was only 6.5 games. Following Denver's recent head-to-head loss to OKC on February 27 (which expanded the deficit), their chances are even lower. The most appropriate probability is 0.02, which is the minimum value strictly allowed by the forecasting interface.
